evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Apply software engineering practices to analytics code, including version control, testing, and continuous integration.
evidence.domain_risk: ensuring our data is clean, accessible, and ready for analysis
evidence.data_team_maturity: Design, build, and maintain clean, scalable, and performance-optimised data models using SQL and dbt.
evidence.jd_authorship: Design, build, and maintain clean, scalable, and performance-optimised data models using SQL and dbt. Apply software engineering practices to analytics code, including version control, testing, and continuous integration.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: ensure our data infrastructure effectively supports self-serve analytics, insightful reporting, and business intelligence across the organisation
evidence.autonomy_level: collaborating closely with Product Analysts, Data Scientists, and Business Stakeholders to meet their analytical and reporting needs
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Design, build, and maintain clean, scalable, and performance-optimised data models using SQL and dbt.

velocity_vs_rigour_quote: Apply software engineering practices to analytics code, including version control, testing, and continuous integration.
velocity_vs_rigour_reasoning: The responsibilities emphasize testing, version control, continuous integration, data validation, and monitoring—core rigour practices—with no competing velocity signals in the job description.
domain_risk_quote: ensuring our data is clean, accessible, and ready for analysis
domain_risk_reasoning: Marketing analytics affects business decisions but lacks regulatory/compliance language and explicit financial reporting requirements.
data_team_maturity_quote: Design, build, and maintain clean, scalable, and performance-optimised data models using SQL and dbt.
data_team_maturity_reasoning: Named tools (dbt, Snowflake/BigQuery/Redshift, Dagster/Airflow) in responsibilities indicate existing data stack requiring maintenance and optimization.
jd_authorship_quote: Design, build, and maintain clean, scalable, and performance-optimised data models using SQL and dbt. Apply software engineering practices to analytics code, including version control, testing, and continuous integration.
jd_authorship_reasoning: This specificity about dbt models, SQL, software engineering practices, and designated domain ownership reflects hands-on technical knowledge only someone doing this work would articulate.
stakeholder_orientation_quote: ensure our data infrastructure effectively supports self-serve analytics, insightful reporting, and business intelligence across the organisation
stakeholder_orientation_reasoning: The primary mission is building and maintaining data infrastructure for internal analytical consumption, not serving commercial, product, or finance specifically.
autonomy_level_quote: collaborating closely with Product Analysts, Data Scientists, and Business Stakeholders to meet their analytical and reporting needs
autonomy_level_reasoning: The role is framed around meeting defined needs of others rather than setting direction or defining analytics strategy.
greenfield_vs_fix_quote: Design, build, and maintain clean, scalable, and performance-optimised data models using SQL and dbt.
ai_role_quote: Apply software engineering practices to analytics code, including version control, testing, and continuous integration.
ai_role_reasoning: Lists software engineering practices without mentioning AI tools or infrastructure requirements.
testing_framing_quote: Apply software engineering practices to analytics code, including version control, testing, and continuous integration.
testing_framing_reasoning: Candidate owns testing as explicit software engineering practice.
loss_aversion_framing_quote: ensuring our data is clean, accessible, and ready for analysis
loss_aversion_framing_reasoning: Protective framing around data quality indicates operational reliability concern.
